Container.Name: unraid-monitor-bot
Container.Overview: A Telegram bot for monitoring Docker containers and Unraid servers. Get real-time alerts, check container status, view logs, and control containers — all from Telegram. Supports AI-powered diagnostics via Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, or local Ollama models. After starting, send /start to your bot to run the interactive setup wizard.

Config.Name: Config directory
Config.Description: Stores config.yaml and the runtime .env file.

Config.Name: Data directory
Config.Description: Stores persistent state (mutes, ignores, model selection).

Config.Name: Docker socket
Config.Description: Required for container monitoring. Read-only access is sufficient.

Config.Name: TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS
Config.Description: Comma-separated Telegram user IDs allowed to use the bot. Get yours from @userinfobot.

Config.Name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Config.Description: (Optional) Anthropic API key — enables AI features via Claude models.

Config.Name: OPENAI_API_KEY
Config.Description: (Optional) OpenAI API key — enables AI features via GPT models.

Config.Name: OLLAMA_HOST
Config.Description: (Optional) Ollama server URL for local AI models, e.g. http://192.168.1.100:11434

Config.Name: DEFAULT_MODEL
Config.Description: (Optional) Override the default AI model. Examples: qwen2.5:7b (Ollama), claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (Anthropic), gpt-4o (OpenAI). Leave blank to use the built-in default for whichever provider is configured.

Config.Name: UNRAID_API_KEY
Config.Description: (Optional) Unraid API key — enables /server, /array, /disks monitoring commands.
